New bugs from nimbus. Verify fix, push: F-003 · 🟡 medium · logic: Policy approval handler does not guard against a stale approvedPolicySha256 echo in the 202-accepted flow, allowing local state to overwrite a newer server-approved policy hash.
Fix: Before applying the optimistic local approvedPolicy + approvedPolicySha256 path, verify that the 202 response body’s reviewId matches the current reviewId and that no newer review payload has been loaded. Also, when a follow-up GET /api/reviews/:id completes, prefer the server’s approvedPolicySha256 over any optimistic local value. For example, include the current review?.updatedAt or a local version counter in state and only apply the optimistic update if it still matches; otherwise, discard it and rely on the next poll result.

packages/report-ui/src/components/ReportPage.tsx L838–905
F-004 · 🔵 low · logic: Policy derivation polling and progress interval timers are not cleared when the review transitions out of policy_pending, which can cause redundant polling and unnecessary state updates.
Fix: Update the useEffect dependencies and guards to clear intervals as soon as review.status leaves policy_pending. One simple approach is to depend on review?.status and, inside the effect, capture the current status in a local variable; if it changes before the next tick, bail early. Alternatively, track a policyDerivationActive boolean and include it in the dependency list, and when it becomes false, avoid scheduling further intervals.

  Goal:
  Fully wrap up Slice 3 from the Review Studio implementation plan. Do not start Slice 4 or Slice 5 work except
  where small supporting changes are strictly required to finish Slice 3 cleanly.

  - `/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/docs/architecture/review-studio-experience.md`
    Use this as the UX intent source if implementation details are ambiguous

  What Slice 3 is supposed to accomplish:
  - Policy states are rendered as distinct user-visible route states
  - `policy_pending` shows progress without forcing a route change
  - `policy_ready` allows review/edit/approval on the same route
  - `policy_approved` transitions cleanly into queue/execution state on the same route
  - refresh/restart preserves policy-stage route recovery
  - cancellation/abandonment behavior is explicit and does not create hidden duplicate runs

  Important current-state observation:
  The repo already has substantial Home/New Review work and active-run work in progress, but policy handling is
  still split across a separate policy route/page and report route/page. Slice 3 is not fully complete until the
  pre-run policy states are handled on the same Review Run route, per the plan.
